original_hypothesis.title: PairFactory.setReferralFee and setCustomReferralFee can DoS all swaps if set > 10000 (underflow in Pair fee update)
original_hypothesis.hypothesis_description: Pair._update0/_update1 compute referralFee = amount * maxRef / 10000 and then do amount -= referralFee. If PairFactory.MAX_REFERRAL_FEE or customReferralFees[pair] exceeds 10000, referralFee becomes > amount and the subtraction underflows, reverting every swap. PairFactory.setReferralFee has no bound check; setCustomReferralFee also has no bound check. A compromised or malicious feeManager can halt all trading across all pairs (global DoS).
original_hypothesis.investigation_rationale: Phase A invariant INV-ACCESS-001 states a direct invariant break with a concrete failure mode. This is operationally critical even if it requires privileged access.

proof_of_signal.pattern: function setReferralFee(uint256 _refFee) external onlyManager { MAX_REFERRAL_FEE = _refFee; } // no bounds; Pair._update0/_update1 does amount -= (amount*maxRef/10000)

preconditions: Attacker controls feeManager or can call onlyManager functions
referral fee is set to > 10000 (or customReferralFees[pair] > 10000)
Pairs continue to route swap fees through _update0/_update1 (normal swaps)

initial_operational_evidence.suspicious_workflow: No bounds check on referral fee setter; arithmetic underflow in fee subtraction
initial_operational_evidence.exploitation_window: Immediately after parameter update; persists until corrected

operational_questions: Is feeManager secured (multisig, timelock, role separation)?
Are there monitoring alerts for out-of-range fee updates?
Can customReferralFees be set per-pair and overlooked operationally?
validation_requirements: Unit test: setReferralFee(10001) then attempt swap; confirm revert
Enumerate all setter call paths and role assignment practices from deployment
Add invariant monitoring on-chain/off-chain for referral fee bounds

preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_1: Acquire feeManager control (role takeover/compromise outside scope) or exploit misassignment
preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_3: Observe global swap failures across pairs
preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_4: Optionally extort for rollback or cause reputational damage

potential_operational_impact.administrative_compromise: Full operational control over trading availability (via DoS)
potential_operational_impact.operational_disruption: All swaps revert; trading halts protocol-wide
potential_operational_impact.recovery_difficulty: Low to medium (requires privileged rollback; markets may be damaged)

investigation_priorities.on_chain_signals: FeeManager calls changing MAX_REFERRAL_FEE or customReferralFees to values > 10000
investigation_priorities.operational_anomalies: Sudden widespread swap reverts with underflow in Pair._update0/_update1
investigation_priorities.behavioral_patterns: Fee updates immediately followed by trading outage

validation_result.impact_summary: Filtered before validation: attack_realism is LOW
validation_result.detailed_analysis: This hypothesis was auto-rejected because its attack_realism grade is LOW. Preconditions require actions outside the attacker's control (off-chain compromise, victim cooperation, or admin collusion). No LLM validation was performed.
